meta.name: Sourav Garg
meta.title: Sourav Garg — Software Developer
meta.description: Backend engineer building Go microservices, inventory sync, and startup products — internships at SeedsAI and Creatvise, full-time at Omniful.

hero.headline: Building backend systems that scale under pressure.
hero.roleLine: Software developer · Go & Python · Omniful · 1+ years
hero.subheadline: Backend engineer at Omniful with startup internships in AI calling and cloud microservices. I ship Go services, sync pipelines, and side projects that real users touch.

projectsSection.title: Projects
projectsSection.subtitle: Side projects and shipped products — from AI roasts to plagiarism detection and real-time chat.

about.title: About
about.subtitle: From intern orbits to full-time flight.
about.openTo: Open to backend and full-stack engineering roles at product startups and growth-stage teams.
body: I'm a software developer focused on backend systems at startups — currently at Omniful working on inventory sync, rate limiting, and WMS integrations in Go. Before that I interned at SeedsAI on AI calling pipelines, Creatvise on OAuth2 microservices, Arcadia Hospitality on full-stack features, and Luneblaze on React and QA.

B.Tech in Computer Engineering from YMCA University of Science & Technology (CGPA 8.3). Joint Secretary of Manan, the coding society, where I helped run workshops for 500+ students. Outside work I build projects like Github Roaster, LC Police, and BingeChat.

projects.title: Github Roaster
projects.summary: AI platform that humorously roasts GitHub profiles using LLaMA — deployed on AWS with global reach on launch day.
projects.imageAlt: Github Roaster AI web interface preview
body: Developers enjoy playful feedback on their public GitHub profiles, but most tools are either dry analytics or generic — nothing that combines entertainment with a memorable first impression.

I built an AI roasting platform leveraging LLaMA to critique GitHub profiles with humor, wrapped in a web interface deployed on AWS EC2 with Nginx and SSL — co-created with Nakshatra Chandna for an overnight launch push.
The launch attracted 6,000 developers worldwide on the first day, proving strong product-market fit for a fun, shareable developer experience.

projects.title: LC Police
projects.summary: AI-assisted plagiarism detection for competitive programming — LLM code normalization plus a custom model for side-by-side evidence.
projects.imageAlt: LC Police plagiarism detection interface preview
body: Competitive programming in India faces widespread solution leaking and copy-paste cheating — honest coders need evidence-backed tools beyond simple API skip checkers.

LC Police strips redundant functions, loops, and comments with LLaMA-3-90B prompt engineering, then scores plagiarism with an in-house model. Users search a profile and get side-by-side code comparison with one-click reporting flows.
The beta launch drew 14.5K page views and 3.5K visitors in 15 hours — built with Dhruv Mehta and the LC Police team as a community tool to push back against contest cheating.

projects.title: BingeChat
projects.summary: Random video chat platform built with Go, WebRTC, and React — live traffic from day one.
body: Random video chat products need low-latency signaling, reliable WebRTC pairing, and a backend that stays observable when traffic spikes on launch day.
I architected BingeChat with Golang and Gin on the backend, WebSockets and WebRTC for real-time video, and React on the frontend — applying OOP design patterns and structured Zap logging throughout.
The platform reached 2,500+ impressions on day one, with logging improvements that cut bug detection time by roughly 70% during early iterations.

projects.title: Go Music
projects.summary: Cross-platform CLI for searching, downloading, and playing YouTube music — fast scraper, clean architecture.
body: Music lovers who prefer the terminal lacked a fast, cohesive Go tool for search, download, and playback without juggling multiple scripts.
I created a cross-platform CLI in Golang with a bare-metal YouTube scraper, concurrency control, file caching, and audio codec support — refactoring with SOLID principles and design patterns.
Search completes in under 200 milliseconds and code redundancy dropped by about 30%, making the tool responsive and maintainable for ongoing feature work.

experienceSection.title: Experience
experienceSection.subtitle: Internships and full-time roles at early-stage startups — from React QA to inventory at scale.

experience.summary: Backend engineer on Omniful's supply-chain platform — WMS integrations, inventory sync, and client APIs in Go (intern Jan–Jul 2025, full-time from Jul 2025).
highlights: Resolved 300+ bugs to improve data consistency and reduce errors across production systems.
Built a cache-backed dynamic rate limiter on client APIs — 40% lower server load with DB-controlled limits per client and faster limit updates under traffic.
Refactored the external WMS integration module with SOLID principles — 20% lower technical debt and 50% less code for future integrations.
Designed a generic inventory sync flow from external WMS into Omniful — 20% less dev effort per new integration and faster onboarding for large catalogs and hub networks.
Enhanced real-time inventory sync with a distributed caching layer — 30%+ fewer database and inter-service API calls with better response latency.
Architected horizontally scalable inventory sync for 1000+ warehouses and 100K+ SKUs per client, resolving prior scalability bottlenecks.
Added structured logging and alerting, cutting issue lookup time by 40–60% and improving system reliability.

experience.summary: AI calling pipeline and real-time agent features at an AI startup.
highlights: Optimized the AI calling pipeline, reducing agent response latency by up to 2 seconds.
Shipped automatic call transfer and API hits during live AI agent calls.

experience.summary: Full-stack features for hospitality operations and guest-facing flows.
highlights: Built REST APIs and integrated frontend dashboards for hospitality booking workflows.
Shipped React UI components wired to backend services for internal operations teams.

experience.summary: Cloud microservices and OAuth2 authentication for a product startup.
highlights: Designed a scalable cloud file-upload microservice in Go, reducing infrastructure costs by 35%.
Engineered an OAuth2 authorization microservice deployed on Google Cloud.
Implemented cookie-based OAuth2 with Next.js serverless functions for stronger backend security.

experience.summary: QA and React development for a product startup — manual testing through component delivery.
highlights: Started as QA intern (Dec 2022–Feb 2023): expanded manual and regression test coverage for React apps.
Transitioned to React developer (Feb–Aug 2023): built UI components and fixed production bugs.
